title: Tapping to See Sleep as Renewal
subtitle: A daytime tapping practice for releasing the idea that rest is giving up on the day
excerpt: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the belief that sleep is retreat — stopping, losing, failing to make the most of the day. The reframe moves toward sleep as connection: the act that brings you back to yourself and to life.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 27 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use during the day when resistance to going to bed is building, or when the idea of sleep feels like wasted time or a concession to tiredness.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the pattern of treating sleep as failure: the sense that going to bed is giving up on the day, retreating, losing ground. The reframe moves toward sleep as the act that reconnects you to life — not a pause from it.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to think sleep meant giving up on the day, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though rest felt like retreat, I honour the part of me that equated stopping with losing.
Even though I'm unlearning that now, I'm open to seeing sleep as connection to life, not separation.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: Sleep isn't an ending; it's a renewal.
Side of eye: It's how nature reconnects me to myself.
Under eye: I don't need to fear the pause.
Under nose: Every living thing rests — why not me?
Chin: Rest is an act of belonging to the natural world.
Collarbone: I'm a participant in that rhythm, not an exception.
Under arm: Sleep keeps me connected to life itself.
Top of head: I honour that connection every night.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Sleep restores my link to the world.
Side of eye: It's how I recharge my place in it.
Under eye: I belong to this cycle of light and dark.
Under nose: Rest is participation, not withdrawal.
Chin: I'm proud to be in rhythm with nature.
Collarbone: My rest blesses everyone I love.
Under arm: I give the world a calmer version of me.
Top of head: I sleep as an act of reconnection.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 27 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
